page: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance belonging to the DPSv5 family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The architecture lacks simultaneous multithreading, providing exactly one thread per physical core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for network-attached storage configurations. There are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results place the D32ps_v5 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1822.98, while the multi-core score reaches 57217.14. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 2231.53 GB/sec, dropping to 170.09 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 77.1 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 8.84 GB/sec, but scales to an average 72.84 GB/sec in multi-threaded execution. The instance demonstrates solid database capabilities with a PassMark database score of 20048.29 and Redis SET operations reaching 41.25 million operations per second. Large language model inference is a notable bottleneck, with 70B parameter models yielding poor performance at 2.31 tokens per second for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a power-efficient and resource-dense alternative to traditional x86 instances for scale-out workloads. The D32ps_v5 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated physical cores and balanced memory allocation. It is less suitable for single-threaded legacy applications or large-scale AI model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance from the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it supports Premium Storage for network-attached disks. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 57217.14 and a PassMark database score of 20048.29. It also achieves 41.25 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. The D32ps_v5 is highly suitable for scale-out workloads, multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving, offering a cost-effective and power-efficient alternative to traditional x86 hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads and databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores and 128 GB RAM, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and balanced memory for efficient scale-out workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated physical ARM64 Ampere Altra cores at 3.0 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
No bundled local storage, with support for Premium Storage network volumes
Strong multi-threaded performance with 57,217.14 stress-ng multi-core score
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and scale-out applications
